1	{
2	  config,
3	  lib,
4	  pkgs,
5	  ...
6	}:
7	
8	{
9	  imports = [
10	    ./hardware-configuration.nix
11	  ];
12	
13	  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
14	  # Bootloader
15	  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
16	  boot.loader.systemd-boot.enable = true;
17	  boot.loader.efi.canTouchEfiVariables = true;
18	
19	  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
20	  # Networking
21	  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
22	  networking.hostId = "8425e349"; # Required for ZFS
23	  networking.networkmanager.enable = true;
24	
25	  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	  # Hardware - NVIDIA
27	  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
28	  nixpkgs.config.allowUnfree = true;
29	  hardware.graphics.enable = true;
30	
31	  services.xserver.videoDrivers = [ "nvidia" ];
32	  hardware.nvidia = {
33	    modesetting.enable = true;
34	    powerManagement.enable = false;
35	    powerManagement.finegrained = false;
36	    open = false;
37	    nvidiaSettings = true;
38	    package = config.boot.kernelPackages.nvidiaPackages.stable;
39	  };
40	
41	  # Force-load NVIDIA display modules in initrd so the GPU drives the
42	  # framebuffer from boot. Without this, hardware.nvidia.modesetting.enable
43	  # wasn't enough on driver 595.58.03 + linux-zen 6.19.12 — the kernel
44	  # command line set nvidia-drm.modeset=1 but nothing actually loaded
45	  # nvidia_drm, leaving Hyprland stuck on EFI fb at 1024x768.
46	  boot.initrd.kernelModules = [
47	    "nvidia"
48	    "nvidia_modeset"
49	    "nvidia_drm"
50	  ];
51	
52	  # Allow qemu-libvirtd to access the GPU
53	  users.groups.video.members = [ "qemu-libvirtd" ];
54	  users.groups.render.members = [ "qemu-libvirtd" ];
55	
56	  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
57	  # Storage Support
58	  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
59	  boot.supportedFilesystems = [ "zfs" ];
60	
61	  # Explicitly opt into the safer 26.11+ default. Forced root pool import
62	  # masks real problems (split-brain, missed exports) and risks data loss.
63	  boot.zfs.forceImportRoot = false;
64	
65	  # ZFS upstream's kernelMaxSupportedMajorMinor = "6.19"; the linux-zen
66	  # kernel pulled in by recent flake.lock updates (7.0.3 as of #528) is past
67	  # that, so ZFS 2.4.1 won't compile (configure-time "Cannot build against
68	  # kernel version 7.0.3-zen1"). Pin to stable until nixpkgs ships a ZFS
69	  # release with a higher kernel cap. PR #531 tried setting the broken flag
70	  # to "warn" instead — that silenced eval but didn't fix the actual build,
71	  # since CI dry-run doesn't compile kmods.
72	  #
73	  # sched-ext is upstream in 6.12+, so services.scx still works on stable.
74	  #
75	  # mkOverride 60 wins against the default-priority assignment in
76	  # modules/common/gaming.nix while still losing to the mkForce inside
77	  # the stable-kernel specialisation, preserving that fallback.
78	  boot.kernelPackages = lib.mkOverride 60 pkgs.linuxPackages;
79	
80	  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
81	  # USB stability
82	  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
83	  # Disable USB autosuspend to prevent enumeration timeouts during boot
84	  # (usb 6-3.1.1: device not accepting address, error -62) and Bluetooth
85	  # adapter power-management stalls that cause mouse stuttering.
86	  boot.kernelParams = [ "usbcore.autosuspend=-1" ];
87	
88	}
89

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [ "main" ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ "main" ]

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  fmt:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
        with:
          persist-credentials: false
      - uses: cachix/install-nix-action@08dcb3a5e62fa31e2da3d490afc4176ef55ecd72 # v30
        with:
          nix_path: nixpkgs=channel:nixos-unstable
          extra_nix_config: |
            accept-flake-config = true
            access-tokens = github.com=${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Check Formatting
        run: nix develop -c nixfmt --check .
        env:
          NIX_CONFIG: "access-tokens = github.com=${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}"

  build-nixos:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        host: [classic-laddie, makers-nix, johnny-walker]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
        with:
          persist-credentials: false
      - uses: cachix/install-nix-action@08dcb3a5e62fa31e2da3d490afc4176ef55ecd72 # v30
        with:
          nix_path: nixpkgs=channel:nixos-unstable
          extra_nix_config: |
            accept-flake-config = true
            access-tokens = github.com=${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      
      # We use --dry-run to verify the derivation can be calculated and dependencies resolved.
      # Full builds might be too heavy for standard GitHub Runners without caching.
      - name: Build ${{ matrix.host }} (Dry Run)
        run: nix build .#nixosConfigurations.${{ matrix.host }}.config.system.build.toplevel --dry-run
        env:
          NIX_CONFIG: "access-tokens = github.com=${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}"

  build-home:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        home: ["personal", "paflynn@bushmills", "patrick@crostini", "paflynn@crostini"]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
        with:
          persist-credentials: false
      - uses: cachix/install-nix-action@08dcb3a5e62fa31e2da3d490afc4176ef55ecd72 # v30
        with:
          nix_path: nixpkgs=channel:nixos-unstable
          extra_nix_config: |
            accept-flake-config = true
            access-tokens = github.com=${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Build ${{ matrix.home }} (Dry Run)
        run: nix build .#homeConfigurations."${{ matrix.home }}".activationPackage --dry-run
        env:
          NIX_CONFIG: "access-tokens = github.com=${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}"

  check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
        with:
          persist-credentials: false
      - uses: cachix/install-nix-action@08dcb3a5e62fa31e2da3d490afc4176ef55ecd72 # v30
        with:
          nix_path: nixpkgs=channel:nixos-unstable
          extra_nix_config: |
            accept-flake-config = true
            access-tokens = github.com=${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    
      - name: Flake Check
        run: nix flake check
        env:
          NIX_CONFIG: "access-tokens = github.com=${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}"

input.new_string:
boot.initrd.kernelModules = [
    "nvidia"
    "nvidia_modeset"
    "nvidia_drm"
  ];

  # Load the CUDA/compute module (nvidia_uvm) explicitly at boot. It only
  # matters at runtime, so boot.kernelModules (not initrd) is correct. We had
  # been relying on udev / nvidia-modprobe to auto-load it on demand, but that
  # path regressed after the 595.80 driver bump (udev failed to mknod
  # /dev/nvidia*), so nvidia_uvm never loaded and CUDA silently broke while the
  # display modules above kept working.
  boot.kernelModules = [ "nvidia_uvm" ];

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
hosts: pin nvidia_uvm in boot.kernelModules on RTX 4090 hosts

Load the CUDA/compute module (nvidia_uvm) explicitly at boot on both 4090
hosts instead of relying on udev/nvidia-modprobe to auto-load it on demand.

On classic-laddie, an automated flake.lock driver bump (595.71.05 -> 595.80)
regressed the udev on-demand load path: udev failed to mknod /dev/nvidia*,
so nvidia_uvm never loaded and /dev/nvidia-uvm never appeared. Display
modules kept working (force-loaded in initrd), but CUDA silently went away,
Ollama fell back to CPU, and a 20GB model loaded into RAM and OOM-locked the
machine.

Pinning nvidia_uvm in boot.kernelModules (runtime, not initrd) makes the
compute module load deterministically at boot. weller is the other 4090 host
with the same latent gap.
EOF
git log --oneline -1

input.command:
gh pr create --reviewer patflynn --title "hosts: pin nvidia_uvm in boot.kernelModules on RTX 4090 hosts" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Problem

On host `classic-laddie` (RTX 4090), CUDA compute silently broke after an automated `flake.lock` update bumped the NVIDIA driver 595.71.05 -> 595.80. Root-cause chain confirmed from journald:

- The driver's **display** modules (`nvidia`, `nvidia_modeset`, `nvidia_drm`) load fine — they are force-loaded in initrd — so the desktop and `nvidia-smi` keep working.
- `nvidia_uvm` (the Unified Virtual Memory module CUDA requires) was relying on udev / `nvidia-modprobe` to load on demand and create `/dev/nvidia-uvm`. The new driver revision regressed that path (udev logged `mknod /dev/nvidiactl ... failed with exit code 1`), so `nvidia_uvm` never loaded and `/dev/nvidia-uvm` never appeared.
- With no usable CUDA device, Ollama fell back to CPU (`library=cpu`, `total_vram="0 B"`) and loaded a 20GB model into 31GB of system RAM, exhausting RAM during the tensor repack step and hard-locking the machine.

## Fix

Pin `nvidia_uvm` in `boot.kernelModules` on both RTX 4090 hosts so the compute module loads deterministically at boot instead of depending on the flaky udev auto-load. `boot.kernelModules` (not initrd) is correct — UVM is only needed at runtime for CUDA, not for the early-boot framebuffer.

- `hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix`: add `boot.kernelModules = [ "nvidia_uvm" ];`
- `hosts/weller/hardware.nix`: append `"nvidia_uvm"` to the existing `boot.kernelModules` list (the other 4090 host, same latent gap)

The initrd display-module block, driver package selection, and everything else are untouched.

## Verification

- `nixfmt --check` on both files: passed
- `nix build --no-link .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel --dry-run`: evaluated, resolved derivations, no errors
- `nix build --no-link .#nixosConfigurations.weller.config.system.build.toplevel --dry-run`: evaluated, resolved derivations, no errors

Run: 20260613-1405-9ff7e689
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

content.text:
Done. PR created: https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/565

## Summary

Pinned the `nvidia_uvm` CUDA/compute module in `boot.kernelModules` on both RTX 4090 NixOS hosts so it loads deterministically at boot instead of depending on the udev/`nvidia-modprobe` on-demand path that regressed after the 595.80 driver bump.

**Changes (additive only):**
- `hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix` — added a new `boot.kernelModules = [ "nvidia_uvm" ];` block next to the existing initrd display-module block, with a comment explaining the root cause.
- `hosts/weller/hardware.nix` — appended `"nvidia_uvm"` to the existing `[ "kvm-amd" ]` list with an inline comment.

The initrd display-module block, driver package selection, and everything else are untouched.

**Verification (all run in this worktree):**
- `nixfmt --check` on both files → passed
- `nix build --no-link .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel --dry-run` → evaluated and resolved derivations, no errors
- `nix build --no-link .#nixosConfigurations.weller.config.system.build.toplevel --dry-run` → evaluated and resolved derivations, no errors
- `klaus _pre-review` → all checks passed, no findings
- pre-commit hooks (detect-private-keys, nixfmt) → passed

PR set with reviewer `patflynn`, footer `Run: 20260613-1405-9ff7e689`, and no AI attribution per the repo's CLAUDE.md.

One note: the CI build matrix in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` covers `classic-laddie` but not `weller`, so weller's eval isn't gated by CI — that's why I ran the dry-run for it locally and confirmed it's clean.

result:
Done. PR created: https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/565

## Summary

Pinned the `nvidia_uvm` CUDA/compute module in `boot.kernelModules` on both RTX 4090 NixOS hosts so it loads deterministically at boot instead of depending on the udev/`nvidia-modprobe` on-demand path that regressed after the 595.80 driver bump.

**Changes (additive only):**
- `hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix` — added a new `boot.kernelModules = [ "nvidia_uvm" ];` block next to the existing initrd display-module block, with a comment explaining the root cause.
- `hosts/weller/hardware.nix` — appended `"nvidia_uvm"` to the existing `[ "kvm-amd" ]` list with an inline comment.

The initrd display-module block, driver package selection, and everything else are untouched.

**Verification (all run in this worktree):**
- `nixfmt --check` on both files → passed
- `nix build --no-link .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel --dry-run` → evaluated and resolved derivations, no errors
- `nix build --no-link .#nixosConfigurations.weller.config.system.build.toplevel --dry-run` → evaluated and resolved derivations, no errors
- `klaus _pre-review` → all checks passed, no findings
- pre-commit hooks (detect-private-keys, nixfmt) → passed

PR set with reviewer `patflynn`, footer `Run: 20260613-1405-9ff7e689`, and no AI attribution per the repo's CLAUDE.md.

One note: the CI build matrix in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` covers `classic-laddie` but not `weller`, so weller's eval isn't gated by CI — that's why I ran the dry-run for it locally and confirmed it's clean.
